problem: Validate market interest in an AI-powered job-application assistant that auto-drafts cover letters and tracks application status — in 2 weeks with a solo developer.
primaryUser: Early-adopter job seekers (25-40, tech roles); solo developer maintains it
successCriteria: User can sign up, paste a job description, get a tailored cover letter draft in < 5 seconds, and track up to 50 applications on a Kanban board; deploys to Vercel; user can share via waitlist link.

stories.title: Auth (Magic Link)
stories.description: Next.js + NextAuth.js with Resend magic-link email; sessions stored in Supabase; only social email providers (Google OAuth) as fallback. No passwords.

stories.title: Cover Letter Generator
stories.description: Paste job description + optional resume text → OpenAI GPT-4o streams a tailored cover letter; user can regenerate or edit inline; saved to DB.

stories.title: Application Tracker (Kanban)
stories.description: Simple drag-and-drop board (react-beautiful-dnd) with columns: Saved, Applied, Interview, Offer, Rejected; CRUD backed by Supabase table; no real-time needed in MVP.

stories.title: Waitlist & Landing Page
stories.description: Marketing landing page on Next.js with product demo GIF, feature list, waitlist email form (stored in Supabase); OG image for social sharing.

stackConstraints: Next.js 14 App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind, Supabase (auth + DB), OpenAI API, Vercel hosting; must ship in 2 weeks — no over-engineering
externalIntegrations: OpenAI GPT-4o, Resend (magic link), Google OAuth, Vercel, Supabase
openQuestions: Rate-limit cover letter generation per user (prevent abuse before paid tier)?
Should the waitlist page be the root / or /waitlist while app is at /app?

modelRecommendation.estimatedCostNote: Estimated $0.30–$0.60 — MVP grade uses Haiku where possible to keep plan cost minimal
